mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 5-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns show no statistical relationship with FEDFUNDS changes.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses above 5.0%, labor market deterioration triggers recession expectations that compress corporate earnings forecasts and drive negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in greater than 65% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 5.0%, or the signal loses predictive power during non-recession periods.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts confirms broad-based consumer demand strength, which directly supports corporate revenue growth and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive returns when either series diverges, or the interaction term shows negative out-of-sample IC over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production signals improving manufacturing capacity utilization and supply-side momentum, which generates positive short-term S&P 500 returns during periods of stable inflation.
failure_condition: INDPRO growth fails to predict positive 5-day returns when CPIAUCSL exceeds 3.0% year-over-year, or the signal reverses direction during high volatility regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly GDP growth above trend confirms sustained aggregate demand expansion, which supports earnings revisions and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth shows zero correlation with 60-day forward returns across multiple business cycles, or Rank IC remains below 0.01 over 10-year samples.

mechanism_narrative: Rapid monthly inflation acceleration forces expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, which increases discount rates and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: SPX delivers positive 20-day returns in greater than 70% of months where CPIAUCSL rises above 0.4%, or the signal loses significance when FEDFUNDS is already above 3.0%.

mechanism_narrative: Strong nonfarm payroll growth combined with a declining federal funds rate creates a soft landing environment that boosts equity valuations and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: The combined signal produces negative returns when PAYEMS growth exceeds 250k while FEDFUNDS rises, or the interaction term shows no predictive alpha over rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month retail sales growth above 0.5% confirms resilient consumer spending, which directly translates to positive short-term S&P 500 returns during periods of low market volatility.
failure_condition: RSAFS growth fails to predict positive 5-day returns when VIX exceeds 20, or the signal shows negative IC during high volatility periods.
